checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=87455000 (2011-01-01)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=435386000 (2014-03-29), jump coincides with VION merger closing
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=371392000 (2016-01-02) vs SalesRevenueNet=3397446000 (FY2015) - both AR and revenue declined together post-merger, moving in proportion

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=701637000 (2013-12-28) rising to Goodwill=1476541000 (2014-03-29) due to VION merger purchase accounting
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=588664000 (2013-12-28) rising to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=1054608000 (2014-03-29)

Goodwill=1233102000 and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=782349000 (2016-01-02) vs SalesRevenueNet=3397446000 (FY2015) - combined soft assets as % of revenue declined from ~72% (FY2013) to ~59% (FY2015)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=78531000 (2015-01-04 to 2016-01-02) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=421348000 (2015-01-04 to 2016-01-02)
DepreciationAndAmortization=269904000 (2015-01-04 to 2016-01-02) largely explains the NI/OCF gap via non-cash add-back

NetIncomeLoss=64215000 (2013-12-29 to 2015-01-03) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=275172000 (2013-12-29 to 2015-01-03), with DepreciationAndAmortization=269517000 covering most of the gap

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold=20200000 (2012-12-30 to 2013-03-30) vs SalesRevenueNet=465657000 (2012-12-30 to 2013-03-30) - COGS tag appears inconsistent/partial for this and several adjacent quarters, precluding reliable margin trend analysis
CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold=14900000 (2013-12-29 to 2014-03-29) vs SalesRevenueNet=946292000 (2013-12-29 to 2014-03-29) - similarly anomalous small COGS relative to revenue

InventoryNet=344583000 (2016-01-02) vs SalesRevenueNet=3397446000 (FY2015 annual) - annual COGS/revenue derived gross margin fell from ~26% (FY2012-13) to ~21% (FY2014-15), plausibly tied to lower commodity prices and VION product mix

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=109000 (2015-01-04 to 2015-04-04); NetIncomeLoss=-9087000 (2015-07-05 to 2015-10-03); NetIncomeLoss=84429000 (2015-10-04 to 2016-01-02) - large swings including a near-zero quarter and a loss quarter indicate genuine earnings volatility, not smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=10835000 (2015-01-03) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=409779000 (2015-01-03), a ratio of ~2.6%

AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=9732000 (2016-01-02) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=371392000 (2016-01-02), maintaining a similar ~2.6% ratio as both AR and allowance declined together

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2016-03-01 (accession 0000916540-16-000038) and 10-Q filed 2016-08-11 (accession 0000916540-16-000052), both filed on standard schedule with no NT filings in the 2010-2016 window
10-K/A filing_date 2006-08-18 and 10-K/A filing_date 2006-10-19 occurred well before the 2010-2016 review period and are not indicative of current reporting quality

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data spanning fiscal 2007 through Q2 2016 (through Assets=4859894000 (2016-07-02)) across balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow line items from 10-K, 10-Q and 8-K filings

top_signals: Massive, discontinuous balance sheet and income statement jumps (e.g., Assets=1713635000 (2013-09-28) to Assets=5549859000 (2014-03-29)) are almost entirely attributable to disclosed acquisitions (Griffin 2011, Rothsay 2013, VION merger 2014) rather than unexplained anomalies, but the scale and complexity of purchase accounting for these deals (large goodwill/intangible recognition, e.g., Goodwill=1476541000 (2014-03-29)) creates elevated inherent risk around valuation, purchase price allocation, and future impairment judgments that warrants continued monitoring.

Operating cash flow persistently and increasingly exceeds net income (NetIncomeLoss=78531000 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=421348000 for FY2015), though this is largely explained by high D&A from purchase accounting (DepreciationAndAmortization=269904000 FY2015) and working-capital releases amid falling commodity prices - a plausible but non-trivial gap worth tracking.

An apparent XBRL tagging inconsistency in CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold for several 2013-2014 quarters (values as low as ~$14.9-20.2M against revenue of $450M-$1,031M in the same quarters) reduces confidence in clean quarter-level margin analysis and should be treated as a data-quality caveat rather than a fraud signal.

No late filings (NT forms) or restatement amendments (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) occurred within the 2010-2016 review window; historical 10-K/A amendments in 2006 predate the review period and do not indicate current-period irregularity.
